seoTitle: How to Use the ClickUp API: Beginner Setup Guide
metaDescription: Get started with the ClickUp API by generating access tokens, structuring requests, and testing calls to create and fetch tasks programmatically.
intro: If you want to automate ClickUp with scripts or connect it to other tools, you'll need to work through its API. This guide covers finding your developer settings, generating a personal access token, and sending your first authenticated request. It's aimed at anyone comfortable with basic scripting who wants to start building against ClickUp's endpoints.

steps.heading: Open your developer settings
steps.body: Go to your ClickUp profile, open account settings, and find the Apps or developer section where API credentials are managed.

steps.heading: Generate a personal access token
steps.body: Click Generate under the Personal Token section to create your secret key string for authenticating requests.

steps.heading: Review the API documentation
steps.body: Check ClickUp's current API docs for base URLs and required authentication headers before writing any code.

steps.heading: Structure your request payloads
steps.body: Use correct JSON models when creating, updating, or fetching workspace tasks so the API accepts your calls.

steps.heading: Test calls with Postman or cURL
steps.body: Pass your authorization header and send a request to confirm you get a valid response before building further.

steps.heading: Watch your rate limits
steps.body: Monitor ClickUp's API rate limits and error responses, and keep your access token stored securely.

faq.question: Where do I find my ClickUp API token?
faq.answer: It's generated from your profile settings under the Apps or developer section, in the Personal Token area.

faq.question: Do I need OAuth for a personal script?
faq.answer: For personal scripts a Personal Access Token is usually enough; OAuth2 apps are more relevant when multiple users need to authorize access.

faq.question: What happens if I hit the API rate limit?
faq.answer: You'll get an error response, so it's important to monitor your call volume and handle retries gracefully.

faq.question: Can I use the ClickUp API to create tasks automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, sending a properly structured POST request to the tasks endpoint lets you create tasks programmatically.
